model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 fixed training steps, the current Adam learning rate is likely conservative for this small micro-resnet. Increasing LR should improve early optimization progress and reduce underfitting without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current model is likely capacity-limited under short training. A moderate width increase in convolutional channels and classifier hidden size should improve representation quality while keeping optimization stable and compute overhead small.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last width increase gave a strong gain, indicating remaining under-capacity. A further moderate channel increase should improve feature quality within the same optimization setup while keeping the script simple.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, doubling batch size exposes the model to more unique examples during training, which can improve validation loss without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"After increasing model width and batch size, switching to AdamW can improve optimization/generalization tradeoff by decoupling weight decay while keeping the rest of the tuned schedule unchanged.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW slightly regressed versus the previous best, so reverting to Adam is the simplest high-confidence move to recover the stronger baseline before further exploration.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size 128 and cosine decay, a slightly higher initial learning rate should improve early progress within the fixed 256-step horizon while still annealing for the final evaluation.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With improved feature learning from wider conv layers and higher LR, expanding the classifier bottleneck should reduce underfitting at minimal complexity cost and may further lower validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increasing classifier width hurt validation loss, so reverting to the previously better hidden size restores the stronger, simpler configuration before testing other directions.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"In this short fixed-step regime, current regularization may be slightly strong; reducing weight decay can improve fit while keeping optimization and architecture unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Lowering weight decay slightly worsened validation loss, so reverting to the previous setting restores the stronger baseline with minimal complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Warmup plus cosine improved stability, so a modest increase in peak learning rate may extract more progress within the fixed step budget while keeping the same schedule shape.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher peak learning rate slightly regressed, so reverting to 2e-3 restores the best-performing setting with the warmup+cosine scheduler.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed training steps, increasing batch size can improve data coverage and gradient quality per update; a moderate bump from 128 to 160 may further reduce validation loss without changing model or optimizer design.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The move from 128 to 160 improved loss, suggesting additional benefit from more examples per optimizer step under fixed-step training; increasing to 192 tests that trend with a still-moderate change.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Performance has improved consistently as batch size increased under fixed-step evaluation, so testing 224 is a targeted continuation of the strongest trend while preserving the rest of the tuned setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 240","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size scaling has been the most reliable improvement axis in this fixed-step setting; a small incremental increase to 240 continues that trajectory while limiting additional runtime risk.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 240","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Given consistent gains from larger batch sizes in this fixed-step regime, one final increase to 256 is the highest-probability last-step improvement while keeping all other strong settings unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
